global_context.scene_description: A dramatic, cinematic close-up photograph of an old, weathered rooftop HVAC package unit shot in moody blue-tinted lighting like a crime scene or forensic photograph. The AC unit is visibly aged — rusted panels, dust-caked vents, dented housing, discolored metal. A bright red tag or sticker is attached to the unit reading 'CONDEMNED' in bold black text, like an official inspection tag. A thin wisp of white smoke or steam rises from a gap in the housing. On the concrete pad below the unit, there is a small puddle of water/condensation with an oily sheen. The background is a dark night sky with minimal ambient light — just enough deep blue to separate the unit from pure black. A single harsh spotlight or flashlight beam illuminates the unit from the upper left, creating dramatic shadows on the right side and making the rust and damage highly visible. Small dust particles are visible floating in the light beam. The mood is ominous, foreboding — this unit is on death's door. Styled like a true crime documentary still or a movie poster for a thriller.
global_context.time_of_day: Night, dark, single artificial light source
global_context.weather_atmosphere: Still, slightly hazy from dust/heat, ominous

lighting.source: Single harsh spotlight or flashlight beam from upper left
lighting.direction: Upper left to lower right, creating strong directional shadows
lighting.color_temp: Cool blue overall with warm highlights on rust spots

composition.camera_angle: Slightly low angle, looking up at the unit — making it loom ominously
composition.framing: Medium-close, unit fills 60% of frame, dark space around it
composition.depth_of_field: Medium-shallow — unit sharp, background falls to dark blur
composition.focal_point: The red CONDEMNED tag on the unit, with the deteriorating housing surrounding it

semantic_relationships: The spotlight beam illuminates the unit from upper left, creating dramatic shadows on the right
The CONDEMNED tag is the sharpest, most colorful element — drawing the eye first against the aged metal
Steam rising from the unit suggests it's overheating or failing
The oily puddle below suggests a leak — physical evidence of failure
Dust particles floating in the spotlight beam create a forensic/investigative atmosphere
The low camera angle makes the dying unit loom over the viewer ominously
The contrast between the fresh red tag and the aged unit tells the story: this has been evaluated and it's done
